heading: Search System Memory
body:
Monitor the total amount of RAM used by your MongoDB Vector Search indexes. Adequate
RAM is critical for MongoDB Vector Search query performance because queries going to
disk are much less performant. Ensure that the entire index is able to
fit in memory.

Ensure that available **System Memory** is always greater than
used **System Memory**. If the index is not frequently queried,
not all of the index might be in memory. Therefore, leverage the
**System Memory** metric in conjunction with the
**Index Size** [Review Available Metrics](review-available-metrics) to
optimize provisioning.

If the vector index size is over 3 GB, we recommend [Quantized vectors](avs-quantization), which stores only 4% of the index
in memory rather than the full index.
